items.title: Community complains of toxic flammable gas leaks in Nigeria's Niger Delta
items.description: In Nigeria, residents in the Niger Delta town of Billé have taken to the streets to demand government action over catastrophic pollution. For several months, the community has complained of toxic, flammable gas leaking from the ground, contaminating water and destroying aquatic life – which the people rely upon for their livelihood. It's the latest in a long series of environmental disasters in the Niger Delta, which have largely been blamed on Western oil companies. Report by FRANCE 24 correspondent Sam Olukoya.

items.description: Around 24,000 pilgrims have visited Mount Kailash in China’s Tibet Autonomous Region since May this year with the pilgrimage season set to continue till September; the numbers so far are up from 20,750 last year when the pilgrimage was reopened for Indian devotees after a gap of five years

items.title: Argentina sink 10-man Swiss to set up England World Cup semi-final clash

items.title: India news: Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike tests Modi
items.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuks has been fasting in protest of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan since over two weeks. As his health deteriorates, the pressure piles up on Prime Minister Narendra Modi. DW has more.

items.title: India, Japan review defence ties, prepare ground for 2+2 dialogue later this year
items.description: According to Ministry of Defence, the two sides assessed the progress made since the previous round of talks and reaffirmed their commitment to further strengthening the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership

items.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa beach tests negative for bird flu
items.description: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

items.title: 'Threat to US sovereignty': Washington launches campaign to dismantle ICC
items.description: The United States has launched a campaign to dismantle the International Criminal Court, calling the Hague-based institution “an intolerable threat to US sovereignty.”

items.title: Bastille Day: France celebrates Ukraine and its allies with military parade
items.description: France celebrates its national holiday today, celebrating the taking of the Bastille in 1789 that kickstarted the French Revolution. As ever, it is being marked by a massive military parade. The focus this year is France's rearmaments and support for Ukraine. 500 coalition troops, including some from Ukraine, will take part in the parade. France 24's Luke Shrago is on the ground at the Champs Elysees and tells us more about what we can expect from the parade.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Argentina and England renew fierce World Cup rivalry in semi-final

items.title: Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30 as police probe possible negligence

items.description: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

items.title: Middle East live: Explosions heard near Hormuz port city Bandar Abbas
items.description: Iranian state television on Tuesday reported that five explosions were heard near the city of Bandar Abbas close to the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday. The port city has been at the centre of renewed fighting which saw the US launch a fresh salvo of strikes against Iran early Tuesday, even as President Donald Trump said a deal with Tehran was still possible. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Gilgit-Baltistan: Pakistan's tourist jewel tested by development and climate change
items.description: In just a few decades, the Hunza Valley in Gilgit-Baltistan, a remote mountainous region of northern Pakistan, has undergone a dramatic transformation. Expanded access to education, improved healthcare and strong local development have reshaped the area, in a model of progress often linked to foundations associated with the Aga Khan, the spiritual leader of the Ismaili community. The minority counts nearly 500,000 members in Pakistan and makes up around 90% of Hunza's population.

items.description: Ukrainian drone strikes have brought shipping in the Sea of Azov to a near standstill, disrupting a corridor that carries up to a quarter of Russia's wheat exports and sending tremors through global grain and oil markets. They have also knocked out a significant share of Russia's refining capacity, prompting Moscow to ban diesel exports until the end of July and further unsettling global oil markets. In Russian-occupied Crimea, petrol prices have reached a record high.

items.description: As France battles a wildfire south of Paris, Stefan Doerr, Director of the Centre for Wildfire Research at Swansea University, tells FRANCE 24's Monte Francis that "we need to prepare the environment more to reduce the risk of fire, but we will never eliminate it completely". Regarding the regeneration of the forest, Doerr said it could take the area up to 30 years to recover.

Houthis launch missiles at Saudi Arabia, in retaliation for Sanaa airport strikes
items.description: Clashes have resumed in Yemen, with Houthis rebels blaming Saudi Arabia and the country's internationally recognised government for strikes on Sanaa Airport. The hits were designed to prevent an Iranian plane, carrying a Houthis delegation, from landing. Story by Ellen Gainsford.

items.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
items.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.

items.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
items.description: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

items.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
items.description: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

items.title: Rescued Oyo principal recounts how kidnappers forced pupils, teachers through nightly treks to evade troops
items.description: "They (the kidnappers) carried the youngest three children. The secondary school girls carried some of the smaller pupils, while the others had to walk. We fell many times," she said. The post Rescued Oyo principal recounts how kidnappers forced pupils, teachers through nightly treks to evade troops appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Staff shortage led to woman being strangled by wheelchair belt, court hears
items.description: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

items.title: Google probed by Swiss regulator over Android default search feature
'This landscape is completely charred': Inside the village at epicentre of Spain's wildfires
items.description: The BBC's Nick Beake sees homes razed to the ground and "a sea of black" in Bédar.

items.title: Ex-navy commander convicted over 2017 ARA San Juan submarine tragedy
items.description: Submarine force commander Claudio Villamide handed three-year suspended sentence for negligence and breach of duties; Three other Navy officers acquitted. Leer más

items.title: Man charged with murder over death in Victoria's east
items.description: A 43-year-old man has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of a 58-year-old Morwell man in Victoria's east at the weekend.

items.title: India's LTM expects AI revenue to outpace traditional services, CEO says
Yemen's Houthis launch missiles at Saudi Arabia after strikes on Sanaa airport
items.description: The Iran-backed Houthis blame Saudi Arabia for the attack in Yemen's capital and say they targeted Abha airport in response.

items.title: Mundial trumps Trump
items.description: ‘The Donald’ had bullied obsequious FIFA president Gianni Infantino into nixing a red card for the home team’s top scorer – an obscene subordination of the beautiful game to power politics. Leer más

items.title: India-Australia ‘administrative arrangements’ for uranium supplies | Explained
items.description: The recent agreement paves the way for Australian uranium exports to India, allowing private sector collaboration under stringent IAEA oversight

items.title: Scaloni admits Argentina 'suffered' in Switzerland win

items.title: Lock Petti to become latest Argentina centurion in Nations Championship Test
items.description: Guido Petti will become the sixth player to reach 100 caps for the Pumas when he starts against Wales in the Nations Championship this weekend in San Juan Leer más

items.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
items.description: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

items.title: Legal action dropped against Ann Marie Smith's NDIS provider
items.description: Federal Court proceedings have been dropped against the former company responsible for caring for South Australian woman Ann Marie Smith, who died six years ago.

items.title: Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook
items.description: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

items.title: Scaloni wants 'never-say-die' legacy for his Argentina team
items.description: National team coach says supporting the team is about "passion" and that he wants his side to be remembered as a collective that "never gave up." Leer más

items.title: Pollution from Musk’s unpermitted xAI power project hits hardest in Black communities
Venezuela: families continue digging through the rubble for relatives
items.description: Venezuelans continue working day in and day out in the rubble to recover their relatives' bodies almost 20 days after twin earthquakes devastated the country.

items.title: Unbreakable Albiceleste propel Lionel Messi's final World Cup mission
items.description: No team had ever recovered from a 2-0 deficit with so little time remaining in a World Cup match, but Argentina refused to let Messi's tournament story come to an end. Leer más

items.title: Kenya: MSF trains health workers to be sent to Ebola-hit DR Congo
items.description: An Ebola training facility set up by Doctors Without Borders in Kenya is a chance for health workers to prepare for one of the most complex emergencies they are ever likely to face.

items.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
items.description: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

items.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
items.description: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

items.title: South Korean court suspends FTC designation Coupang founder Kim Bom as controlling entity
Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
items.description: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

items.title: Spy agency warns active Russian hackers targeting Australian industry
items.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

items.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
items.description: Supermarket billionaire Dieter Schwarz is investing billions in his cloud company, Schwarz Digits, to rival Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

items.title: One Indian sailor killed, six wounded as Iranian missiles strike oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Missiles hit the Mombasa and Al Bahiyah in ​Omani territorial waters; fires damaged both tankers but crews brought them under control

items.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian strike on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz that set the container ship ablaze and forced its crew to abandon it.

items.title: China, Russia conclude joint naval drills in Yellow Sea
items.description: The ships' crews practiced joint maneuvering, anti-submarine missions, combat against enemy surface ships, and rescue operations, Russian Defence Ministry said

items.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
items.description: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

items.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
items.description: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

items.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
items.description: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

items.title: Sydney radio ratings reveal KIIS on the slide without Kyle and Jackie O
items.description: The KIIS Sydney audience has slipped further in the radio ratings in the latest blow to ARN after the departure of renowned breakfast radio juggernaut Kyle and Jackie O.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: EU bans gold imports from Sudan to curb money financing the war
items.description: The ban also restricts exports of mercury and cyanide to Sudan, chemicals widely used in gold mining.

items.title: Weather or human error? What led to Vietnam boat tragedy? | Explained
items.description: What caused the boat carrying Indian tourists in Vietnam to capsize? Were there any violations? What action has Vietnam taken?

items.description: If we have learned anything by now, though, it is that you simply cannot write this team off. Leer más

items.title: Gaza Strip: EU conference pledges €900m in donations
items.description: The conference in Brussels was attended by Palestinian representatives. The EU's top diplomat said the bloc's support depends on reforms in the Palestinian Authority.

items.title: Death toll in Bangkok bar fire reaches 30
items.description: As Bangkok kicks-off an investigation into a fire in a bar last Sunday, three people among the wounded have died. The number of deaths from the blaze now stand at 30.

items.title: Nine foreign nationals, including French woman, among 13 victims of Spain's deadly wildfire
items.description: At least thirteen people, including five Britons, three Belgians and one national each from France and Spain, were confirmed killed in one of Spain's deadliest wildfires in recent years, authorities said Monday. The blaze in the southeastern province of Almeria near the Mediterranean coast destroyed rural settlements and forced investigators to seek genetic information from suspected victims' relatives for identification.

items.title: King’s Swan Upping on the Thames shows healthy cygnets but rising litter
items.description: Traditional skiffs glided along the River Thames during Swan Upping as crews counted and weighed cygnets, reporting encouraging numbers while warning about the growing threat of river litter.

items.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
items.description: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

items.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
items.description: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

items.title: New York becomes the first state to impose a data center moratorium
Ukraine, 9 countries form ballistic missile defense coalition
items.description: Ukraine and nine European countries launched a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, drawing on Kyiv’s wartime experience.

items.title: Lebanon and Israel hold talks in Rome amid renewed West Asia fighting
items.description: According to a Lebanese diplomatic source familiar with the content of the talks, “the Lebanese army is ready to gradually take control of the localities from which the Israeli army would withdraw”

items.title: A Gaza bookseller returned to the ruins for the books
items.description: When war destroyed his home and bookstore in Beit Lahia, Mohammed Saad lost far more than a business.

items.title: Singapore's economy grew 5.7% in Q2, easing from 6.3% in previous quarter: Advance estimates
items.description: The manufacturing sector grew 12.2 per cent in the April to June period, compared with the same months a year ago.

items.title: Court rejects Ganduje’s request to compel PREMIUM TIMES reporter to disclose sources on port ownership report
items.description: Justice Simon Amobeda of the Federal High Court in Kano ruled that while the court possesses the authority to subpoena a journalist to testify, the reporter retains the right to protect their sources unless those sources are directly linked to the case. The post Court rejects Ganduje’s request to compel PREMIUM TIMES reporter to disclose sources on port ownership report appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .
